contaminant.name: Mineral Staining
contaminant.description: Mineral-stain contamination, it arises from dissolved iron, manganese, and copper in water sources, which precipitate as tenacious reddish-brown or blackish deposits upon evaporation or oxidation, distinguishing itself from organic soiling by its inorganic hardness and metallic sheen. On porous materials like limestone or grout, these stains penetrate deeply, manifesting persistent discoloration that resists superficial abrasion, whereas on denser surfaces such as granite, they form superficial crusts. Removal challenges stem from strong adhesion influenced from substrate chemistry, yet laser cleaning proves effective, as the process ablates layers selectively, preserving the underlying integrity without chemical residues.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Manganese Oxide generation detected - low toxicity risk
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings
